channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump threatens to try to take back the Panama Canal. Panama's president balks at the suggestion
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Donald Trump suggested Sunday that his new administration could try to regain control of the Panama Canal that the United States “foolishly” ceded to its Central American ally, contending that shippers are charged “ridiculous” fees to pass through the vital transportation channel link
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden commutes sentences of 37 federal death row inmates in final month of presidency
item.description: President Biden is commuting the sentences for 37 of the 40 convicted murderers on federal death row, reclassifying them to life in prison without parole.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Review by Senate Democrats finds more unreported luxury trips by Clarence Thomas
item.description: A report by Democrats on the Judiciary Committee found additional travel taken in 2021 by Thomas but not reported on his annual financial disclosure, including trips on private jets and a yacht trip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump pushes back on 'President Musk' claims
item.description: “No, he’s not gonna be president, that I can tell you,” Trump said. “And I’m safe, you know why? He can’t be, he wasn’t born in this country.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Democrats condemn ethical lapses by Thomas, Supreme Court justices
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Judiciary Committee condemned the Supreme Court for an “ethical crisis of its own making” in a new report released Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Senate review of Supreme Court ethics finds more luxury trips and urges enforceable code of conduct
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A nearly two-year investigation by Democratic senators of Supreme Court ethics details more luxury travel by Justice Clarence Thomas and urges Congress to establish a way to enforce a new code of conduct.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate passes bill to boost Social Security benefits for some
item.description: The Senate voted overwhelmingly in the early hours of Saturday to pass legislation to boost Social Security benefits for more than 2 million Americans.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Closures, Social Security checks, furloughs: What a government shutdown might mean
item.description: Congress has until midnight Friday to come up with a way to fund the government or federal agencies will shut down, meaning hundreds of thousands of federal employees could be sent home — or stay on the job without pay — just ahead of the holidays.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Government funding bill clears Congress and heads to President Biden, averting a shutdown
item.description: The Senate has rushed through final passage of a bipartisan plan to fund federal operations and prevent a government shutdown. It now goes to President Joe Biden, who is expected to quickly sign it into law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s reelection victory pushes some LGTBQ Minneapolis residents to safety training
item.description: For some marginalized communities the second Trump term is fraught with fears over personal safety. In Minneapolis, one organization is helping with de-escalation services and gun training.
item.dc:creator: Odette Yousef, MPR News

item.title: Trump backs plan B spending deal, urges swift passage
item.description: “All Republicans, and even the Democrats, should do what is best for our Country, and vote ‘YES’ for this Bill, TONIGHT!“ Trump posted on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Fani Willis disqualified as prosecutor on Trump Georgia election case
item.description: A Georgia appeals court booted Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis's (D) office from the 2020 election interference case against President-elect Trump on Thursday due to her relationship with a top prosecutor on the case.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Trump, Vance call for clean CR, debt ceiling hike, urge GOP to sink Johnson compromise
item.description: President-elect Trump and Vice President-elect Vance on Wednesday called for Republicans to approve a clean stopgap funding bill paired with a hike to the debt ceiling, saying the previous government-funding measure negotiated by Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) should be torpedoed.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: In last-ditch effort, Democrats push to get Julie Su confirmed as labor secretary
item.description: Acting Secretary Julie Su has led the Labor Department for nearly two years, despite never getting a Senate confirmation vote. With time running out, her staunchest supporters haven't given up.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Nevada's 6 Electoral College votes certified for President-elect Donald Trump
item.description: Nevada Republican Party Chairman Michael McDonald officially certified the state's six Electoral College votes for President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday during a gathering on the UNR campus in Reno.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Horsford vows to defend Nevada families as priority for government cuts grows
item.description: Nevada Democratic Rep. Steven Horsford is doing more than just serving as a watchdog on Republican initiatives to cut government bureaucracy -- he's participating in the process.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida GOP lawmaker files bill banning political flags in public buildings
item.description: If one state senator can shore up enough support in Tallahassee, political flags will no longer be allowed to be flown in public buildings.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge rules Trump's conviction withstands Supreme Court immunity decision
item.description: A New York judge upheld a jury’s verdict that convicted President-elect Trump of a felony, ruling the outcome of the hush money case can withstand the Supreme Court’s new test for presidential immunity.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Former FBI informant pleads guilty to fabricating Biden claims
item.description: A former FBI informant on Monday entered a guilty plea on four federal charges related to falsifying statements that in part spurred a GOP congressional investigation into the Biden family.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams' former chief advisor expects to be indicted: lawyer
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ingrid Lewis-Martin, a former top aide to New York Mayor Eric Adams, expects to be indicted on criminal charges related to alleged improper gifts, her lawyer said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams denied public funds for 2025 reelection campaign: CFB
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams was denied public funds for his 2025 reelection campaign, the New York City Campaign Finance Board announced on Monday. The CFB cited the federal charges against Adams as one of the reasons why it denied him the money.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11
